name: The Old Switchback
descriptionSeed: The old road up toward the high pass — a series of stone-cut switchbacks clinging to the mountainside, older than the transition and repaired a hundred times since. A single ruined waystation marks the halfway point, roof half-gone, hearth still usable. Travelers between the Roost, the wilds, and the country beyond all thread this trail, which makes it a place of chance meetings and chance troubles: weather, footing, and whoever else is on the road.
encounterFlavor: The exposed road — a sudden squall, a rockfall, a stranger sheltering at the waystation who may be company or trouble, a view that stops you cold, the pass ahead deciding whether to let anyone through.
questSeeds: Greta keeps the halfway waystation alone and has for years; she's seen everyone who's crossed and remembers what they carried — her memory is a resource and, to the wrong person, a threat
Something has been taking travelers' packs on the upper switchback — not bandits, something that leaves the food and takes the strange objects
A caravan is stranded by a slide and will miss the pass before snow unless the trail is cleared or another way found
axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
worldPosNote: Near-centre folk country — the Valley sits where all axes cross faintly, so longitude spreads and colatitude stays low. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
